title: Enabling project-wide SSH keys to access VM instances is security-sensitive
description: Enabling project-wide SSH keys allows any user with a key in the project metadata to access all VM instances, increasing the risk of unauthorized access. Instance-level SSH keys provide better control and auditability. Project-wide keys should be avoided unless absolutely necessary.
message: Project-wide SSH keys are enabled. Use instance-level keys for better security.
impact: Project-wide keys can be abused to access all VMs, leading to potential compromise of the entire environment.
recommendation: Disable project-wide SSH keys. Use instance-level keys and manage access through IAM and OS Login.

noncompliant.description: Project-wide SSH keys are set, granting access to all VMs.

compliant.description: No project-wide SSH keys are set; access is managed per instance.

why_is_this_an_issue: Project-wide SSH keys make it difficult to control and audit access, increasing the risk of compromise.
how_to_fix: Remove project-wide SSH keys from project metadata. Use instance-level keys and enable OS Login for centralized access management.

security_mappings.CWE: CWE-732: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
